categories.title: Working Instance Lifecycle
categories.summary: Make generated Mole instances easier to validate, compare with source, and upgrade conservatively.
categories.priority_notes: This aligns with the current product goal of making working instances credible beyond initial scaffolding.

tasks.title: Add machine-readable upgrade ownership classes
tasks.description: Introduce a conservative manifest that classifies repository paths as safe-copy, merge-carefully, or never-overwrite for future upgrade reporting.
acceptance_criteria: The manifest is documented in a readable format.
Core directories are assigned ownership classes.
User-owned evidence, context, and raw folders are marked never-overwrite.
A test or validation step confirms the manifest can be parsed.

tasks.title: Implement check-updates report scaffold
tasks.description: Replace the placeholder check-updates output with a report that compares source version to instance metadata and lists likely next actions.
acceptance_criteria: A test covers check-updates output for an up-to-date instance.
A test covers check-updates output when source is newer than the instance.
The report lists safe additions separately from manual review items.

categories.title: Command Surface
categories.summary: Make outcome-oriented Mole commands more consistent and useful without overbuilding the backend.
categories.priority_notes: This supports adoption, but follows the instance lifecycle work.

tasks.title: Normalize CLI naming and help output
tasks.description: Review the CLI help and command output for consistent Mole naming, command examples, and next-step language.
acceptance_criteria: Help output consistently uses Mole and `mole` terminology.
A test or snapshot protects the main help output.
No command behaviour changes beyond text output.
